title: Romantic Glassy Japanese Masarepa Bake
description: A sweet and savory Japanese-inspired dish featuring a glassy-jelly masarepa crust with a fermented kimchi filling.
instructions: In a medium bowl, mix together the masarepa, coconut sugar, and salt until well combined.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, yuzu, and water until smooth.
Gradually add the yuzu mixture to the masarepa mixture and mix until a thick dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes pliable and smooth.
Divide the dough into two equal pieces and roll out each piece into a thin sheet, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place one sheet of the dough into the bottom of a 9x9 inch baking dish, pressing it into the corners to fit.
Spread the ferment-kimchi evenly over the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Place the second sheet of the dough on top of the kimchi, pressing it gently to seal the edges.
Bake the masarepa bake for 25 minutes or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set.
Remove the bake from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before slicing it into squares.
Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and chopped cilantro before serving.
Cover and refrigerate for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months for later use.
